Since the lake starts with the maximum population of 100 tons, if all fishermen catch a large amount, the fish population will drastically decrease, reducing future income for everyone. To maintain a sustainable fish population and ensure steady income over the long run, it is best to catch a moderate amount.

If I catch too much, the fish population will drop significantly, and future catches will be lower. If I catch too little, I might miss out on immediate income. A balanced approach is to catch around 10 tons, assuming others do the same, so the total catch is about 50 tons, leaving 50 tons in the lake. After reproduction, the fish population will return to 100 tons, sustaining future income.

Answer: 10

Since the lake starts with the maximum population of 100 tons, if everyone fishes too aggressively this month, the fish population will decline, reducing future income. To optimize long-term income, I should fish a moderate amount to allow the fish population to sustain or grow after reproduction.

If I fish too little, I miss out on immediate income; if I fish too much, the population will drop. Assuming the other fishermen also want to sustain the population, a cooperative approach would be to fish a total of around 50 tons this month (10 tons each on average). This will leave 50 tons before reproduction, which will double to 100 tons again, maintaining the population.

Therefore, I will catch 10 tons this month.
